bio: A Reubenite who rebelled with Korah and Dathan against Moses. He and his household were swallowed by the earth as divine punishment.

name: Abishag
bio: A beautiful young Shunammite woman who cared for the aging King David. After David's death, Adonijah asked Bathsheba to request her as his wife, which Solomon interpreted as a bid for the throne.

name: Achaicus
bio: A Corinthian believer who visited Paul alongside Stephanas and Fortunatus, supplying what was lacking from the Corinthian church.

name: Adah
bio: One of the two wives of Lamech (descendant of Cain). She bore Jabal, the father of tent-dwellers and cattle keepers, and Jubal, the father of musicians (Moses 5:44-45).

name: Adoni-bezek
bio: A Canaanite king captured by the tribes of Judah and Simeon. They cut off his thumbs and big toes, and he acknowledged that God had repaid him for doing the same to seventy kings.

name: Adoni-zedek
bio: King of Jerusalem who formed a coalition of five Amorite kings against Gibeon for making peace with Israel. Joshua defeated them, and Adoni-zedek was captured and executed.

name: Adonijah
bio: Fourth son of David who attempted to seize the throne as David lay dying. His claim was thwarted by Nathan, Bathsheba, and Zadok, who secured Solomon's coronation. He was later executed by Solomon after requesting David's concubine Abishag as a wife.

name: Aeneas
bio: A man in Lydda who had been bedridden with palsy for eight years. Peter healed him saying, 'Jesus Christ maketh thee whole,' and he arose immediately.

name: Aha
bio: Son of the Nephite chief captain Zoram, mentioned when Alma inquired of the Lord about where to find the Lamanite armies.

name: Ahah
bio: Jaredite king, son of Seth (a Jaredite, not the biblical Seth). He did obtain the kingdom and did do all manner of iniquity in his days.

name: Ahaziah (king of Judah)
bio: Son of Jehoram and Athaliah who reigned one year as king of Judah. He visited the wounded Joram of Israel and was killed by Jehu during Jehu's purge of the house of Ahab.

name: Ahikam
bio: Son of Shaphan who protected Jeremiah from being put to death after his temple sermon. His son Gedaliah became governor after the fall of Jerusalem.

name: Ahimelech
bio: Priest at Nob who gave David the holy bread and the sword of Goliath when David was fleeing from Saul. Saul had him and the other priests killed by Doeg the Edomite for aiding David.

name: Akish
bio: A wicked Jaredite who formed a secret combination to obtain power. He conspired to kill King Omer in order to marry Jared's daughter. He became king but his secret combinations led to devastating civil wars among the Jaredites.

name: Alexander (son of Simon)
bio: Son of Simon of Cyrene and brother of Rufus, mentioned in Mark 15:21 when Simon was compelled to carry Jesus' cross.

name: Alexander (the coppersmith)
bio: A coppersmith who did Paul 'much evil' according to 2 Timothy 4:14. Possibly the same Alexander delivered to Satan alongside Hymenaeus in 1 Timothy 1:20.

name: Almon W. Babbitt
bio: Early Latter-day Saint mentioned in D&C 124 with whom the Lord was not well pleased, as he aspired to establish his own counsel instead of the ordained counsel of the Presidency.

name: Alpheus Cutler
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council. He later organized his own splinter group.

name: Amaleki
bio: Son of Abinadom and the final writer on the small plates of Nephi. He recorded the reign of King Benjamin and encouraged all people to come unto Christ. He had no seed and entrusted the plates to King Benjamin.

name: Amaron
bio: Son of Omni who briefly kept the Nephite records. He recorded that the more wicked Nephites were destroyed according to the Lord's word. He gave the plates to his brother Chemish.

name: Amasa
bio: David's nephew whom Absalom appointed as commander of his rebel army. After Absalom's defeat, David named Amasa commander to replace Joab. Joab murdered Amasa by stabbing him while pretending to greet him.

name: Amasa Lyman
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:136 as a counselor to the president of the high priests quorum, and in D&C 136 to organize a company for the westward trek. He later served as an apostle.

name: Amgid
bio: Jaredite king who obtained the kingdom by force. He held the kingdom until Com son of Coriantum drew away half of it from him.

name: Aminadab
bio: A Nephite dissenter living among the Lamanites who witnessed Nephi (son of Helaman) and his brother Lehi surrounded by fire while in prison. He recognized the divine manifestation and told the other Lamanites to repent, leading to the conversion of about 300 people.

name: Aminadi
bio: An ancestor of Amulek who was notable for interpreting writing on the wall of the temple that was written by the finger of God. He was a descendant of Nephi, son of Lehi, and of Joseph who was sold into Egypt.

name: Amlici
bio: A cunning Nephite who sought to become king after the transition to the reign of judges. He gathered followers and led a rebellion. Though defeated by the Nephites, he allied with the Lamanites and attacked again before being killed by Alma the Younger in personal combat.

name: Ammah
bio: One of the missionary companions of Aaron son of Mosiah who preached to the Lamanites. He was imprisoned in the land of Middoni along with Muloki.

name: Ammaron
bio: Brother of Amos, a Nephite record keeper who hid the sacred records in the hill Shim. He instructed the young Mormon, at age ten, to take the records when he was old enough and continue the record of his people.

name: Ammon
bio: A strong Nephite, descendant of Zarahemla, who led an expedition from Zarahemla to find the people of Zeniff. He discovered King Limhi's people in bondage to the Lamanites. He is distinct from Ammon the son of Mosiah.

name: Amnigaddah
bio: Jaredite descendant in the lineage between Aaron and Coriantum, mentioned in the genealogy of Ether. He lived in captivity.

name: Amnor
bio: One of four Nephite spies sent by Alma to watch the camp of the Amlicites. He and his companions reported back on the combined Amlicite-Lamanite army.

name: Amoron
bio: A Nephite mentioned in Mormon's epistle to Moroni. According to Mormon, Amoron and others took Lamanite women prisoners and subjected them to abuse during the final Nephite-Lamanite wars.

name: Amos (son of Amos)
bio: Son of Amos who kept the sacred records after his father. He maintained the records until about AD 305 when he delivered them to his brother Ammaron.

name: Amos Davies
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to pay stock into the Nauvoo House and to hearken to the counsel of Joseph Smith.

name: Amplias
bio: A believer in Rome whom Paul greets as 'my beloved in the Lord' in Romans 16:8.

name: Amram
bio: Son of Kohath and grandson of Levi. He married Jochebed, and they were the parents of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.

name: Amulon
bio: Leader of the wicked priests of King Noah who fled into the wilderness after Noah's death. He and the other priests kidnapped Lamanite daughters as wives. He later became a teacher over Alma's people in bondage and persecuted them for praying.

name: Andronicus
bio: Greeted by Paul in Romans 16:7 alongside Junia as his kinsman and fellow prisoner who was 'of note among the apostles' and was in Christ before Paul.

name: Anna
bio: An elderly prophetess of the tribe of Asher who spent her days fasting and praying in the temple. She was present when the infant Jesus was brought to the temple and spoke of him to all who looked for the redemption of Jerusalem.

name: Antiomno
bio: King of the land of Middoni and a friend of King Lamoni. Lamoni persuaded him to release Aaron, Muloki, and Ammah from prison.

name: Antionah
bio: A chief ruler in the city of Ammonihah who questioned Alma about the resurrection and the meaning of the scripture that man should not eat of the tree of life and live forever.

name: Antipus
bio: A Nephite military commander who, with Helaman and the stripling warriors, devised a strategy to lure Lamanite armies out of fortified cities. His forces were weakened from battle but joined the young warriors in defeating the pursuing Lamanites.

name: Apelles
bio: A believer in Rome whom Paul greets as 'approved in Christ' in Romans 16:10.

name: Apollos
bio: An eloquent Alexandrian Jew who was mighty in the scriptures. He preached boldly in Ephesus but knew only the baptism of John until Priscilla and Aquila instructed him more fully. He became a powerful preacher in Corinth, and Paul mentioned him alongside himself as fellow workers.

name: Apphia
bio: A woman addressed in the greeting of the Epistle to Philemon alongside Archippus. Traditionally considered Philemon's wife.

name: Aquila
bio: A Jewish tentmaker from Pontus and husband of Priscilla. He and his wife were expelled from Rome under Claudius and settled in Corinth, where they met and worked with Paul. They traveled with Paul and hosted a church in their home.

name: Araunah
bio: A Jebusite who owned the threshing floor on Mount Moriah where David built an altar to stop a plague. He offered it freely, but David insisted on paying. Solomon later built the temple on this site.

name: Archeantus
bio: Nephite soldier mentioned in Mormon's epistle to his son Moroni. He fell by the sword in a battle against the Lamanites near the end of the Nephite civilization.

name: Archelaus
bio: Son of Herod the Great who reigned over Judaea after his father's death. Joseph was afraid to return there and instead went to Galilee.

name: Archippus
bio: Called Paul's 'fellowsoldier' in Philemon 2 and urged in Colossians 4:17 to 'take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.'

name: Aretas
bio: The Nabataean king whose governor in Damascus guarded the city in an attempt to seize Paul, who escaped by being lowered in a basket through a window in the wall.

name: Aristobulus
bio: A man in Rome whose household Paul greets in Romans 16:10.

name: Artemas
bio: A companion of Paul whom he planned to send to Titus in Crete as a replacement so Titus could join Paul in Nicopolis.

name: Asa Dodds
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to take his journey to the western countries with Calves Wilson to proclaim the gospel.

name: Asahel
bio: Brother of Joab and Abishai, and nephew of David. Renowned for his swiftness, he pursued Abner after the battle of Gibeon, and Abner killed him reluctantly in self-defense.

name: Asenath
bio: Daughter of Potiphera, priest of On. Pharaoh gave her to Joseph as his wife. She bore Manasseh and Ephraim.

name: Asher
bio: Eighth son of Jacob, born to Zilpah (Leah's handmaid). His tribe was promised rich food and royal delicacies. Anna the prophetess in the New Testament was of the tribe of Asher.

name: Ashpenaz
bio: Chief of Nebuchadnezzar's eunuchs who was put in charge of Daniel and his companions. God gave Daniel favor with Ashpenaz, though Ashpenaz feared the king's reaction to their dietary request.

name: Asyncritus
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:14.

name: Barabbas
bio: A prisoner held by the Romans at the time of Jesus' trial. Pilate offered the crowd a choice between releasing Jesus or Barabbas, and the crowd demanded Barabbas be freed. He was described as a robber and insurrectionist who had committed murder.

name: Barak
bio: An Israelite military commander whom the prophetess Deborah directed to lead 10,000 men against the Canaanite general Sisera. He agreed to go only if Deborah accompanied him. The victory was decisive, but the honor of killing Sisera went to Jael.

name: Barzillai
bio: A wealthy and aged Gileadite who provided food and supplies to David during his flight from Absalom. David offered him a place at court in Jerusalem, but Barzillai declined because of his age.

name: Bethuel
bio: Son of Nahor and Milcah, and father of Rebekah and Laban. He consented to Rebekah's marriage to Isaac when Abraham's servant came seeking a wife for his master's son.

name: Bezalel
bio: Son of Uri, of the tribe of Judah. God filled him with his Spirit, with wisdom and skill, and appointed him as chief craftsman for building the tabernacle and its furnishings.

name: Bigthan
bio: One of two eunuchs who guarded King Ahasuerus's threshold and conspired to assassinate the king. Mordecai discovered the plot and reported it through Esther, and both conspirators were hanged.

name: Blastus
bio: Herod Agrippa I's personal chamberlain whom the people of Tyre and Sidon befriended in order to gain an audience with the king and secure peace.

name: Burr Riggs
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to take his journey to the south country with Major N. Ashley.

name: Cainan
bio: Son of Enos (the Sethite patriarch) mentioned in Moses 6:17. He is part of the genealogy from Adam through Seth that leads to Enoch and Noah.

name: Calves Wilson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to take his journey to the western countries with Asa Dodds to proclaim the gospel.

name: Carpus
bio: A man in Troas with whom Paul left his cloak, books, and parchments, which he asks Timothy to bring in 2 Timothy 4:13.

name: Catherine Smith
bio: Sister of Joseph Smith, named in Joseph Smith--History 1:4 as one of the eleven children in the Smith family.

name: Cezoram
bio: A Nephite chief judge who was murdered by secret combination members. His son was also killed while serving as chief judge. Their murders marked a period of increasing Gadianton influence in Nephite government.

name: Charles Anthon
bio: Professor of classical studies at Columbia College in New York City. Martin Harris brought him the transcript of Book of Mormon characters, and he certified the translation was correct but later recanted when he learned the source (Joseph Smith--History 1:64-65).

name: Charles C. Rich
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council. He later became an apostle.

name: Chemish
bio: Brother of Amaron who briefly contributed to the Book of Omni. He recorded that he saw his brother write in the book and passed the record to his son Abinadom.

name: Chilion
bio: Son of Elimelech and Naomi who married Orpah the Moabitess. He died in Moab along with his brother Mahlon.

name: Chloe
bio: A woman whose household members reported to Paul about divisions in the Corinthian church, prompting his discussion in 1 Corinthians 1:11.

name: Chuza
bio: Herod's household steward whose wife Joanna was a follower of Jesus and helped support his ministry financially.

name: Claudia
bio: A believer who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul in 2 Timothy 4:21.

name: Claudius (emperor)
bio: The Roman emperor who commanded all Jews to depart from Rome, which brought Aquila and Priscilla to Corinth. The prophet Agabus foretold a great famine that came in his days.

name: Clement
bio: A fellow laborer of Paul at Philippi mentioned in Philippians 4:3, whose name Paul says is 'in the book of life.'

name: Cohor
bio: Jaredite warrior, son of Noah, who continued his father's rebellion against King Shule. He was slain in battle by Shule.

name: Com
bio: Jaredite king, son of Coriantum. He reigned for 49 years and fought against robbers who infested the land. He drew away half the kingdom from Amgid.

name: Coriantor
bio: Jaredite man, son of Moron, who dwelt in captivity all his days. He was the father of Ether, the last Jaredite prophet.

name: Coriantum
bio: Jaredite king, son of Emer, who reigned in his father's stead. He lived to a great age without children until his wife died; he then married a young maid and had sons and daughters.

name: Coriantumr
bio: A descendant of Zarahemla who, as a Lamanite military commander, led a bold attack straight through Nephite territory to capture Zarahemla. He was eventually surrounded and forced to surrender.

name: Corihor
bio: Jaredite rebel who overthrew his father Kib and took the kingdom by force. He was later overthrown by his brother Shule, who restored the kingdom to Kib.

name: Corom
bio: Jaredite king, son of Levi, who reigned in righteousness and did that which was good all his days. He had many sons and daughters.

name: Crescens
bio: A companion of Paul who departed for Galatia, mentioned in 2 Timothy 4:10 in the same context as Demas leaving for Thessalonica.

name: Crispus
bio: The chief ruler of the synagogue in Corinth who believed in the Lord with all his household. Paul personally baptized him.

name: Cumenihah
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men and fell in battle.

name: Cushan-rishathaim
bio: King of Mesopotamia who oppressed Israel for eight years during the period of the judges. God raised up Othniel, the first judge, to deliver Israel from his hand.

name: Cyrenius
bio: The Roman governor of Syria when the first census was taken that brought Joseph and Mary to Bethlehem.

name: Damaris
bio: A woman in Athens who believed Paul's preaching alongside Dionysius the Areopagite after his sermon on Mars Hill.

name: Dan
bio: Fifth son of Jacob, born to Bilhah (Rachel's handmaid). His tribe settled in the far north of Israel. The city of Dan became one of the places where Jeroboam set up golden calves.

name: Daniel Miles
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:138 to preside over the quorum of seventies.

name: Daniel Stanton
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Seymour Brunson.

name: Dathan
bio: A Reubenite who joined Korah's rebellion against Moses and Aaron, challenging their authority. The earth opened and swallowed him alive along with his household.

name: Daughter of Jared
bio: Unnamed daughter of Jared (son of Omer) who devised a plan to restore her father to the throne by dancing before Akish, leading him to seek Omer's head in exchange for her hand in marriage.

name: David Dort
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: David Fullmer
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: Demetrius (of 3 John)
bio: A man commended by the apostle John in 3 John 12 as having 'good report of all men, and of the truth itself,' in contrast to the problematic Diotrephes.

name: Dinah
bio: Daughter of Jacob and Leah who was violated by Shechem, a Hivite prince. Her brothers Simeon and Levi avenged her by deceiving and massacring the men of Shechem, an act that Jacob condemned.

name: Dionysius the Areopagite
bio: A member of the Athenian court (the Areopagus) who believed Paul's preaching about the resurrection in Athens.

name: Doeg
bio: An Edomite who was chief of Saul's herdsmen. He informed Saul that the priest Ahimelech had helped David. When Saul's guards refused to kill the priests, Doeg slaughtered 85 priests of Nob.

name: Don Carlos Smith
bio: Younger brother of Joseph Smith, named in Joseph Smith--History 1:4. He later served as a church leader and editor of the Times and Seasons before dying in 1841.

name: Dorcas
bio: A disciple in Joppa known for her charitable works. When she died, Peter was summoned, and he raised her from the dead, causing many to believe in the Lord. She is one of the few women in the New Testament explicitly called a disciple.

name: Drusilla
bio: The Jewish wife of Governor Felix. She was present when Felix sent for Paul and heard him speak about faith in Christ, righteousness, temperance, and judgment to come.

name: Dunbar Wilson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: Eden Smith
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Micah B. Welton.

name: Edson Fuller
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey to Missouri with Jacob Scott.

name: Egyptus
bio: Daughter of Ham mentioned in Abraham 1:23-25. Her daughter, also named Egyptus, discovered the land of Egypt. The name means Egypt in the Chaldean language.

name: Elah (king of Israel)
bio: Son of Baasha who reigned as king of Israel for two years. He was assassinated by his chariot commander Zimri while drinking himself drunk at the house of Arza in Tirzah.

name: Eldad
bio: One of the seventy elders chosen by Moses. He and Medad remained in the camp yet the Spirit rested on them and they prophesied. Joshua urged Moses to stop them, but Moses wished all God's people were prophets.

name: Elimelech
bio: Husband of Naomi from Bethlehem who took his family to Moab during a famine. He died there, leaving Naomi with their two sons Mahlon and Chilion, who also later died.

name: Elizabeth Marsh
bio: Not directly named in D&C text. Skipping.

name: Elkanah
bio: Father of the prophet Samuel and husband of Hannah and Peninnah. He was a devout man who made yearly pilgrimages to worship at Shiloh. He supported Hannah when she dedicated their son Samuel to the Lord's service.

name: Elymas
bio: A Jewish false prophet and sorcerer on Cyprus who tried to turn the proconsul Sergius Paulus from the faith. Paul struck him blind temporarily.

name: Emer
bio: Jaredite king, son of Omer, who reigned in righteousness. Under his rule the people prospered greatly and he saw the Son of Righteousness. He lived to 62 years and saw peace in the land.

name: Emer Harris
bio: Early Latter-day Saint and brother of Martin Harris. Called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Simeon Carter.

name: Emron
bio: Nephite soldier mentioned in Mormon's epistle to his son Moroni. He fell by the sword in a battle against the Lamanites near the end of the Nephite civilization.

name: Enoch (son of Cain)
bio: Son of Cain, named in Moses 5:42-43. Not the same as the prophet Enoch who built the city of Zion. Cain named a city after him.

name: Enos (Sethite)
bio: Son of Seth and grandson of Adam, mentioned in the genealogy in Moses 6:13. He lived 905 years and is part of the righteous lineage from Adam to Noah.

name: Enosh
bio: Son of Seth and grandson of Adam. During his time, people began to call upon the name of the LORD.

name: Epaenetus
bio: Greeted by Paul in Romans 16:5 as 'my wellbeloved' and identified as the firstfruits of Achaia (or Asia) unto Christ.

name: Ephron
bio: A Hittite from whom Abraham purchased the cave of Machpelah and surrounding field at Hebron for 400 shekels of silver as a burial place for Sarah.

name: Er
bio: Firstborn son of Judah. He married Tamar but was wicked in the sight of the LORD, who put him to death.

name: Erastus
bio: Called 'the chamberlain of the city' (treasurer of Corinth) in Romans 16:23. Paul sent him to Macedonia with Timothy and later mentions he 'abode at Corinth' in 2 Timothy 4:20.

name: Erastus Snow
bio: Early Latter-day Saint apostle called in D&C 136:12 to organize a company for the westward trek alongside Ezra T. Benson.

name: Esrom
bio: Son of Omer the Jaredite king. He and his brother Coriantumr restored Omer to the throne after the destruction of Akish's kingdom.

name: Ethem
bio: Jaredite king, son of Ahah, who did do that which was wicked in his days. Prophets came warning of destruction, but the people rejected them.

name: Eubulus
bio: A believer who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul in 2 Timothy 4:21.

name: Eunice
bio: Timothy's mother, a Jewish believer praised by Paul for her 'unfeigned faith.' She and her mother Lois nurtured Timothy in the faith from childhood.

name: Euodia
bio: A woman in the Philippian church whom Paul beseeches to be of the same mind as Syntyche, noting that both had labored with him in the gospel.

name: Ezias
bio: A prophet mentioned by Nephi son of Helaman in his discourse, listed alongside Zenock, Isaiah, and Jeremiah as one who testified of the coming of the Son of God.

name: Ezra Booth
bio: Early Latter-day Saint and former Methodist minister called in D&C 52 to journey with Isaac Morley. He later apostatized and published critical letters about the church.

name: Ezra T. Benson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint apostle called in D&C 136:12 to organize a company for the westward trek alongside Erastus Snow. Grandfather of President Ezra Taft Benson.

name: Ezra Thayre
bio: An early Church member who received a personal revelation (D&C 33) calling him to preach the gospel. He was called on a mission but struggled with obedience. He is one of many early members specifically addressed by name in the Doctrine and Covenants.

name: Fortunatus
bio: A Corinthian believer who visited Paul alongside Stephanas and Achaicus, supplying what was lacking from the Corinthian church.

name: Frederick G. Williams
bio: A counselor to Joseph Smith in the First Presidency and one of the early leaders of the Church. He served as a scribe and physician. He is mentioned in several D&C sections and helped compile the 1835 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants.

name: Gad
bio: Seventh son of Jacob, born to Zilpah (Leah's handmaid). His tribe settled east of the Jordan River. Jacob prophesied that a troop would overcome him, but he would overcome at the last.

name: Gad (prophet)
bio: David's seer who twice brought him messages from the LORD. He told David to leave Moab and return to Judah. After David's census, Gad offered him a choice of three punishments.

name: Gaius
bio: A companion of Paul from Macedonia who was seized during the Ephesian riot. Paul also calls a Gaius his host in Corinth (Romans 16:23), and personally baptized a Gaius (1 Corinthians 1:14). Gaius of Derbe is listed in Acts 20:4. The name may refer to multiple individuals.

name: Gaius (of 3 John)
bio: The addressee of 3 John, whom the apostle John praises for walking in truth and showing hospitality to traveling missionaries even though they were strangers.

name: George Miller
bio: Early Latter-day Saint described in D&C 124 as being without guile and trusted for his integrity. He was sealed to the office of bishopric and appointed to help build the Nauvoo House.

name: George W. Harris
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: Gershom
bio: Firstborn son of Moses and Zipporah. Moses named him Gershom, saying 'I have been a sojourner in a foreign land,' reflecting his exile from Egypt in Midian.

name: Geshem
bio: An Arab leader who allied with Sanballat and Tobiah against Nehemiah. He accused Nehemiah of plotting rebellion against Persia to prevent the wall's completion.

name: Gid
bio: Nephite military captain who served under Helaman and Captain Moroni. He was chief captain over the band appointed to guard Lamanite prisoners and helped execute strategic military operations.

name: Giddianhi
bio: Leader of the Gadianton robbers who wrote a threatening letter to Lachoneus, the Nephite governor, demanding surrender. The letter is notable for its flattery and audacity. He led the robber armies against the gathered Nephites and was killed in battle.

name: Giddonah
bio: Father of Amulek, identified in the genealogy of Amulek as a descendant of Aminadi. A separate Giddonah served as high priest in the land of Gideon and confronted Korihor.

name: Gideon Carter
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Sylvester Smith.

name: Gidgiddonah
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men. He fell in battle along with his entire division.

name: Gilgah
bio: One of the four sons of Jared (the Jaredite patriarch). Named alongside Jacom, Mahah, and Orihah.

name: Gilgal
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men and fell in battle.

name: Habakkuk
bio: A prophet who questioned God about why the wicked prosper and why God would use the Babylonians to punish Judah. God responded by assuring that judgment would come. Habakkuk's declaration, 'the just shall live by his faith,' became a key New Testament theme.

name: Haggai
bio: A post-exilic prophet who urged the returned Jewish exiles to rebuild the temple in Jerusalem. He challenged them for living in paneled houses while the Lord's house lay in ruins. His encouragement led to the completion of the Second Temple.

name: Hagoth
bio: A Nephite shipbuilder described as 'an exceedingly curious man.' He built a large ship and sailed northward from the narrow neck of land with many Nephites and provisions. He built other ships as well. Polynesian Latter-day Saints have traditionally connected Hagoth with the ancestors of Pacific Island peoples.

name: Ham
bio: Son of Noah who saw his father's nakedness after the flood, for which his son Canaan was cursed. He became the ancestor of various peoples in Africa and the Near East. He is mentioned in the Pearl of Great Price in connection with his wife Egyptus.

name: Hamor
bio: A Hivite prince and father of Shechem. After his son defiled Dinah, Hamor negotiated with Jacob for intermarriage. He and his son were killed by Simeon and Levi in retaliation.

name: Hanani
bio: Brother of Nehemiah who came from Judah to Susa and reported the broken walls and distress of Jerusalem's remnant. His report moved Nehemiah to weep, fast, and petition the king.

name: Hananiah (false prophet)
bio: A false prophet who publicly broke the yoke Jeremiah wore and prophesied that Babylon's yoke would be broken within two years. Jeremiah declared God would replace the wooden yoke with iron, and Hananiah died within the year.

name: Haran
bio: Son of Terah and brother of Abraham. He was the father of Lot. He died in Ur of the Chaldeans before his father Terah.

name: Haran
bio: Brother of Abraham who died in Ur of the Chaldees during a famine. Father of Lot and Milcah.

name: Harbonah
bio: One of the king's eunuchs who pointed out that Haman had built a gallows to hang Mordecai. The king then ordered Haman hanged on it instead.

name: Harvey Whitlock
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey with David Whitmer, preaching along the way to Missouri.

name: Hearthom
bio: Jaredite king, son of Lib, who lived in captivity all his days after being overthrown.

name: Hegai
bio: The king's eunuch in charge of the harem at Susa. He favored Esther, giving her the best place in the harem and providing her with beauty treatments and special food.

name: Helem
bio: One of three companions of Ammon (the expedition leader) who journeyed from Zarahemla to the land of Nephi to find the people of Zeniff.

name: Helorum
bio: Son of King Benjamin and brother of Mosiah and Helaman. He was taught in the language of his fathers and the prophecies of the holy prophets.

name: Hem
bio: One of three companions of Ammon (the expedition leader) who journeyed from Zarahemla to the land of Nephi to find the people of Zeniff.

name: Heman Basset
bio: Early Latter-day Saint mentioned in D&C 52 who transgressed and had his calling taken from him and placed upon Simonds Ryder.

name: Henry G. Sherwood
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to pay stock into the Nauvoo House and named to the Nauvoo high council.

name: Henry Herriman
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:138 to preside over the quorum of seventies.

name: Hermas
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:14.

name: Hermes
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:14.

name: Hermogenes
bio: Named in 2 Timothy 1:15 alongside Phygellus as one of those in Asia who turned away from Paul.

name: Herodias
bio: Wife of Herod Antipas and granddaughter of Herod the Great. She left her first husband Philip to marry Antipas, which John the Baptist condemned. She orchestrated John's beheading by having her daughter Salome request his head as a reward for her dance.

name: Herodion
bio: A believer in Rome whom Paul greets as 'my kinsman' in Romans 16:11.

name: Heth
bio: Jaredite king who embraced secret combinations and slew his own father to obtain the kingdom. Under his reign a great famine and poisonous serpents plagued the land.

name: Himni
bio: Son of King Mosiah and one of the four missionary sons who preached to the Lamanites. Like his brothers, he had rebelled against the church before being converted by an angelic visitation. He joined the fourteen-year mission to the Lamanites.

name: Hiram (craftsman)
bio: A skilled bronze worker from Tyre, son of a widow of Naphtali. Solomon hired him to cast the two great bronze pillars, the bronze sea, and other furnishings for the temple.

name: Hiram Page
bio: One of the Eight Witnesses of the Book of Mormon. He claimed to receive revelations through a seer stone, but was corrected in D&C 28 that only Joseph Smith was appointed to receive revelations for the church.

name: Hobab
bio: Son of Reuel (Jethro) the Midianite, Moses' brother-in-law. Moses asked him to guide the Israelites through the wilderness because he knew the desert terrain.

name: Hophni
bio: Son of Eli the priest and brother of Phinehas. He was a corrupt priest who profaned the sacrifices. He died alongside his brother when the Philistines captured the ark of the covenant.

name: Hur
bio: He assisted Aaron in holding up Moses' hands during the battle with Amalek at Rephidim, ensuring Israel's victory. He was also left in charge with Aaron when Moses ascended Mount Sinai.

name: Hushai
bio: David's loyal friend and counselor. During Absalom's rebellion, David sent Hushai to feign allegiance to Absalom and counter Ahithophel's advice. His counsel delayed Absalom's pursuit, giving David time to escape.

name: Hymenaeus
bio: A false teacher whom Paul 'delivered unto Satan' for blasphemy in 1 Timothy 1:20. He taught that the resurrection had already occurred, overthrowing the faith of some.

name: Ichabod
bio: Son of Phinehas (son of Eli), born on the day the ark was captured. His mother named him Ichabod, meaning 'The glory has departed from Israel,' and died in childbirth.

name: Irad
bio: Son of Enoch (the descendant of Cain, not the prophet Enoch). He begat Mahujael according to Moses 5:43.

name: Isaac Galland
bio: Called in D&C 124 to put stock into the Nauvoo House. The Lord said he loved him for his work and would forgive his sins. He was appointed and ordained to go with Hyrum Smith.

name: Isaac Hale
bio: Father of Emma Smith, residing in Harmony, Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania. Joseph Smith boarded at his house while employed by Josiah Stoal (Joseph Smith--History 1:56-57).

name: Isaac Morley
bio: Early Latter-day Saint who had attempted a communal living arrangement before joining the church. Called in D&C 52 to journey to Missouri. He served as a counselor in a stake presidency.

name: Isabel
bio: A harlot in the land of Siron who enticed Corianton, son of Alma the Younger, away from his missionary duties among the Zoramites. She is one of only three non-biblical women named in the Book of Mormon. Alma rebuked his son severely for going after her.

name: Isaiah
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his visit to the Americas. He shares the name with the Old Testament prophet but is a distinct individual.

name: Ishmael (son of Nethaniah)
bio: A member of the royal family who assassinated Gedaliah, the governor appointed by Babylon over Judah's remnant, along with Jews and Babylonian soldiers. He fled to the Ammonites.

name: Issachar
bio: Ninth son of Jacob and Leah. His tribe was known for understanding the times and knowing what Israel ought to do.

name: Ittai
bio: A Philistine from Gath who followed David into exile during Absalom's rebellion, despite David urging him to return. David appointed him commander of a third of his army for the decisive battle.

name: Jabal
bio: Son of Lamech (descendant of Cain) and Adah. He was the father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock, the progenitor of nomadic herders.

name: Jacob Scott
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey to Missouri with Edson Fuller.

name: Jacom
bio: One of the four sons of Jared (the Jaredite patriarch). Named alongside Gilgah, Mahah, and Orihah.

name: Jael
bio: Wife of Heber the Kenite who killed the Canaanite general Sisera by driving a tent peg through his temple while he slept in her tent. She is celebrated in the Song of Deborah as "blessed above women."

name: James Covill
bio: A Baptist minister who covenanted with the Lord that he would obey whatever commandment was given. D&C 39 was received for him, but he rejected the word and returned to his former principles as described in D&C 40.

name: James Foster
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:138 to preside over the quorum of seventies.

name: Japheth
bio: Son of Noah who survived the flood. He and his brother Shem covered their father's nakedness by walking backward with a garment. He became the ancestor of various Indo-European peoples.

name: Jared
bio: Father of Enoch in the line from Adam to Noah. He lived 962 years according to Genesis. He is mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis 5 and 1 Chronicles 1, as well as in the Book of Moses in the Pearl of Great Price.

name: Jared Carter
bio: An early Church member who received a revelation (D&C 79) calling him to preach the gospel. He served several missions and was known for his effective proselytizing.

name: Jarom
bio: Son of Enos and author of the Book of Jarom. He briefly recorded that the Nephites prospered by keeping the law of Moses and looking forward to the Messiah. He passed the records to his son Omni.

name: Jason
bio: A believer in Thessalonica who hosted Paul and Silas. An angry mob dragged him before the city rulers accusing him of harboring men who preached 'another king, one Jesus.' He may be the same Jason Paul greets in Romans 16:21.

name: Jehoahaz (king of Israel)
bio: Son of Jehu who reigned 17 years over Israel. He did evil, and God gave Israel into the hand of Hazael and Ben-hadad of Aram. He sought the LORD's favor and was granted a deliverer.

name: Jehoahaz (king of Judah)
bio: Son of Josiah who reigned three months as king of Judah. Pharaoh Neco deposed him, put him in chains, and took him to Egypt where he died.

name: Jehosheba
bio: Daughter of King Joram and wife of Jehoiada the priest. She rescued the infant Joash from Athaliah's massacre of the royal family by hiding him in the temple for six years.

name: Jeneum
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men and fell in battle.

name: Jephthah
bio: A judge of Israel, the son of Gilead by a prostitute, who was initially rejected by his half-brothers. He was called back to lead Israel against the Ammonites. He made a rash vow to sacrifice whatever came out of his house first if victorious — tragically, his daughter came out to greet him.

name: Jeremiah
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his visit to the Americas. He shares the name with the Old Testament prophet but is a distinct individual.

name: Jesse Baker
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:137 to preside over the quorum of elders alongside John A. Hicks and Samuel Williams.

name: Jesus Justus
bio: A Jewish Christian coworker of Paul mentioned in Colossians 4:11 as one of the few circumcised believers who were a comfort to Paul.

name: Joel
bio: A prophet of Judah who used a devastating locust plague as a metaphor for the coming day of the Lord. He prophesied that God would pour out his Spirit on all flesh — a prophecy Peter cited as fulfilled at Pentecost.

name: Johanan
bio: Military leader who warned Gedaliah about Ishmael's plot. After the assassination, Johanan rescued the captives but feared Babylonian reprisal. Against Jeremiah's counsel, he led the remnant to Egypt.

name: John A. Hicks
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:137 to preside over the quorum of elders alongside Samuel Williams and Jesse Baker.

name: John C. Bennett
bio: Assistant president of the Church and first mayor of Nauvoo who was excommunicated for immoral conduct in 1842. He subsequently became a vocal critic of the Church. He is referenced in D&C 124.

name: John Corrill
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 50 to labor in the vineyard. He later served as a church historian in Missouri but eventually left the church.

name: John E. Page
bio: Member of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles named in D&C 124:129. He later left the church and did not follow the main body westward.

name: John Murdock
bio: An early convert whose wife died giving birth to twins. The twins were adopted by Joseph and Emma Smith (who had recently lost twins of their own). He received a revelation in D&C 99 calling him to preach.

name: John Snider
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to help build the Nauvoo House alongside George Miller, Lyman Wight, and Peter Haws.

name: John Whitmer
bio: One of the Eight Witnesses of the Book of Mormon and brother of David Whitmer. He served as the Church's first historian, as commanded in D&C 47. He was excommunicated in 1838 but never denied his testimony of the Book of Mormon.

name: Jonas
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his visit to the Americas following his resurrection.

name: Joseph
bio: Youngest son of Lehi and Sariah, born in the wilderness during the family's journey. Lehi gave him a special blessing referencing the prophecies of Joseph of Egypt about a future seer named Joseph (Joseph Smith) who would come through his lineage.

name: Joseph Barsabas
bio: Nominated alongside Matthias to replace Judas Iscariot as an apostle but not chosen when the lot fell to Matthias. Also surnamed Justus.

name: Joseph Knight Sr.
bio: Early supporter of Joseph Smith who employed him before the translation of the Book of Mormon. Addressed in D&C 23, he was commanded to take up his cross and pray vocally.

name: Joseph Wakefield
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 50 to strengthen the churches and in D&C 52 to take his journey to the eastern lands with Solomon Humphrey.

name: Joseph Young
bio: Elder brother of Brigham Young, called in D&C 124:138 to preside over the quorum of seventies. He served as senior president of the First Quorum of the Seventy.

name: Joseph of Arimathea
bio: A wealthy member of the Jewish council who was a secret disciple of Jesus. After the crucifixion, he asked Pilate for Jesus' body and provided his own new tomb for the burial. He is mentioned in all four Gospels.

name: Joses (brother of Jesus)
bio: Named as one of Jesus' brothers in Matthew 13:55 and Mark 6:3, alongside James, Simon, and Judas.

name: Josiah Butterfield
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:138 to preside over the quorum of seventies.

name: Josiah Stoal
bio: A man in Chenango County who hired young Joseph Smith in 1825 to dig for a silver mine. He was among the earliest believers in Joseph's prophetic calling (Joseph Smith--History 1:56).

name: Jotham (son of Gideon)
bio: Youngest son of Gideon who escaped Abimelech's massacre. He told the parable of the trees choosing a king from atop Mount Gerizim, warning Shechem of judgment for crowning Abimelech, then fled.

name: Jubal
bio: Son of Lamech (descendant of Cain) and Adah. He was the father of all who play the harp and flute, the progenitor of musicians.

name: Judas (of Damascus)
bio: A man on the street called Straight in Damascus at whose house Saul of Tarsus stayed after his blinding vision on the road to Damascus.

name: Judas Barsabas
bio: A prophet in the Jerusalem church sent with Silas to Antioch to deliver the decision of the Jerusalem council. He encouraged and strengthened the believers there.

name: Judas of Galilee
bio: A rebel leader mentioned by Gamaliel in Acts 5:37 who rose up during the days of the Roman census and drew followers after him. He perished and his followers were scattered.

name: Julia
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:15 alongside Philologus.

name: Junia
bio: Greeted by Paul in Romans 16:7 alongside Andronicus as his kinswoman and fellow prisoner, described as 'of note among the apostles' and in Christ before Paul.

name: Kib
bio: Jaredite king, son of Orihah, who was taken captive by his son Corihor but later restored through his son Shule's rebellion against Corihor.

name: Kim
bio: Jaredite king, son of Morianton, who did not reign in righteousness. He was brought into captivity by his brother and remained in captivity all his days.

name: Kimnor
bio: Father of Akish the Jaredite. Akish was described as the son of Kimnor when the daughter of Jared proposed her plan to dance before him.

name: Kish
bio: Jaredite king, son of Corom. He passed away and his son Lib reigned in his stead.

name: Kishkumen
bio: An assassin who murdered the chief judge Pahoran (son of Pahoran) and later attempted to murder Helaman (son of Helaman). He was killed by a servant of Helaman who intercepted him. He was an early leader of the band that Gadianton later took over.

name: Kumen
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his visit to the Americas following his resurrection.

name: Kumenonhi
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his appearance to the Nephites after his resurrection.

name: Lachoneus
bio: Chief judge and governor of the Nephites who received the threatening epistle from Giddianhi. He wisely gathered the entire Nephite nation into one defensible area with provisions, rather than marching out to fight the Gadianton robbers. This strategy led to the robbers' defeat.

name: Lamah
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men and fell in battle.

name: Lamech
bio: Son of Methuselah and father of Noah. He lived 777 years. He named his son Noah, saying, 'This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands.' He is in the direct line from Adam to Noah.

name: Lamech (descendant of Cain)
bio: A descendant of Cain who took two wives, Adah and Zillah. He boasted to his wives about killing a man who wounded him, claiming seventy-sevenfold vengeance.

name: Lehi
bio: A Nephite military commander who served under Captain Moroni during the wars with the Lamanites. He was given command of the city of Mulek and helped in several key battles. He should not be confused with the prophet Lehi, father of Nephi.

name: Lehonti
bio: Leader of a group of Lamanite dissenters on mount Antipas who refused to go to war against the Nephites. He was deceived and poisoned by Amalickiah through a gradual poisoning scheme.

name: Leman Copley
bio: A former Shaker convert who joined the early church. D&C 49 was received in response to his request to understand the errors of the Shakers, and he was called to reason with them.

name: Levi (Jaredite)
bio: Jaredite king, son of Kim, who served in captivity for 42 years before making war and obtaining the kingdom. He did that which was right in the sight of the Lord.

name: Levi W. Hancock
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey with Zebedee Coltrin. Also called in D&C 124 to preside over the quorum of seventies.

name: Lib
bio: A Jaredite king described as a righteous ruler under whom the Jaredites prospered greatly. The land was developed, and the people grew exceedingly rich during his reign.

name: Limhah
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men and fell in battle.

name: Limher
bio: One of four Nephite spies sent by Alma to watch the camp of the Amlicites. He and his companions reported back on the combined Amlicite-Lamanite army.

name: Linus
bio: A believer who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul in 2 Timothy 4:21. Traditionally identified as the first Bishop of Rome after Peter.

name: Lois
bio: Timothy's grandmother, praised by Paul for her 'unfeigned faith' which dwelt first in her and in Timothy's mother Eunice before being passed on to Timothy.

name: Lucius of Cyrene
bio: One of the prophets and teachers in the church at Antioch in Acts 13:1. Possibly the same Lucius whom Paul calls a kinsman in Romans 16:21.

name: Luke Johnson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint and member of the original Quorum of the Twelve Apostles. Called in D&C 68 and D&C 75 to proclaim the gospel to the eastern countries.

name: Luram
bio: Nephite soldier mentioned in Mormon's epistle to his son Moroni. He fell by the sword in a battle against the Lamanites near the end of the Nephite civilization.

name: Lydia
bio: A seller of purple cloth from Thyatira who was Paul's first convert in Europe, in the city of Philippi. She was baptized with her household and insisted Paul and Silas stay at her home, which became a meeting place for the church.

name: Lyman E. Johnson
bio: One of the original members of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles, called in 1835. He was among the youngest of the original Twelve. He later left the Church and was excommunicated in 1838.

name: Lyman Wight
bio: An early apostle and member of Zion's Camp. He is mentioned in D&C 124 as one called to the Quorum of the Twelve. After Joseph Smith's death, he led a group of Saints to Texas rather than follow Brigham Young.

name: Lysanias
bio: The tetrarch of Abilene mentioned in Luke 3:1 as part of the political context when the word of God came to John the Baptist in the wilderness.

name: Mahah
bio: One of the four sons of Jared (the Jaredite patriarch). Named alongside Jacom, Gilgah, and Orihah.

name: Mahalaleel
bio: Son of Cainan in the Sethite genealogy, mentioned in Moses 6:19 and D&C 107:53. He was present at Adam-ondi-Ahman when Adam blessed his posterity.

name: Maher-shalal-hash-baz
bio: Son of Isaiah whose prophetic name means 'Quick to the plunder, swift to the spoil.' His birth was a sign that before he could speak, Assyria would plunder Damascus and Samaria.

name: Mahijah
bio: A man who came to Enoch the prophet and asked him to identify himself and explain where he came from (Moses 6:40).

name: Mahlon
bio: Son of Elimelech and Naomi who married Ruth the Moabitess. He died in Moab, and Ruth later married Boaz as kinsman-redeemer.

name: Mahujael
bio: Descendant of Cain through Irad. He begat Methusael according to Moses 5:43.

name: Major N. Ashley
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to take his journey to the south country with Burr Riggs.

name: Malchus
bio: The high priest's servant whose right ear Peter cut off with a sword during Jesus' arrest in the Garden of Gethsemane. Jesus healed the ear.

name: Manaen
bio: One of the prophets and teachers in the church at Antioch, described as having been brought up with Herod the tetrarch.

name: Manoah
bio: A Danite from Zorah and father of Samson. The angel of the LORD appeared to his wife to announce Samson's birth. Manoah offered a sacrifice on which the angel ascended in the flame.

name: Mary (mother of Mark)
bio: Mother of John Mark whose house in Jerusalem served as a gathering place for believers. Peter went to her house after his miraculous release from prison in Acts 12:12.

name: Mary (of Rome)
bio: A woman in the Roman church whom Paul greets in Romans 16:6, noting that she 'bestowed much labour on us.'

name: Mary (wife of Cleophas)
bio: Stood at the cross of Jesus alongside his mother and Mary Magdalene. Identified as the wife of Cleophas in John 19:25 and possibly 'Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses' in Mark 15:40.

name: Mathoni
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his visit to the Americas. His brother Mathonihah was also among the twelve.

name: Mathonihah
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his appearance to the Nephites. He was the brother of Mathoni.

name: Matthias
bio: Chosen by lot to replace Judas Iscariot as the twelfth apostle after the Ascension. He had been a follower of Jesus from the time of John's baptism. Little else is recorded about his subsequent ministry.

name: Medad
bio: One of the seventy elders chosen by Moses who prophesied in the camp alongside Eldad, even though they had not gone to the tabernacle.

name: Menahem
bio: King of Israel who seized the throne by killing Shallum. He paid heavy tribute to Tiglath-pileser of Assyria and was brutal in warfare.

name: Merab
bio: Elder daughter of King Saul who was promised to David as a wife for killing Goliath. Instead, Saul gave her to Adriel the Meholathite, breaking his promise.

name: Mesha
bio: King of Moab who rebelled against Israel after Ahab's death. When Israel, Judah, and Edom attacked, Mesha sacrificed his firstborn son on the city wall, and the coalition withdrew.

name: Meshach
bio: One of Daniel's three companions cast into the fiery furnace for refusing to worship Nebuchadnezzar's golden image. He emerged unharmed along with Shadrach and Abednego. His Hebrew name was Mishael.

name: Methusael
bio: Descendant of Cain through Mahujael. He begat Lamech (the Cainite Lamech) according to Moses 5:43.

name: Methuselah
bio: Son of Enoch and grandfather of Noah. He is famous as the longest-lived person in the Bible at 969 years. He is mentioned in the lineage from Adam to Noah. In the Pearl of Great Price, he received prophecies from his father Enoch.

name: Micah (of Ephraim)
bio: An Ephraimite who set up a shrine with a carved image and hired a Levite as his personal priest. The tribe of Dan stole his idols and priest while migrating north to conquer Laish.

name: Micah B. Welton
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Eden Smith.

name: Michal
bio: Daughter of King Saul and first wife of David. She helped David escape from Saul by lowering him through a window. Later, she despised David for dancing before the Ark of the Covenant and was told she would have no children.

name: Milcah
bio: Wife of Nahor (Abraham's brother) and daughter of Haran. She was the grandmother of Rebekah, who married Isaac.

name: Milcah
bio: Wife of Nahor and daughter of Haran, named in Abraham 2:2.

name: Mnason
bio: An early disciple from Cyprus who hosted Paul and his companions when they traveled from Caesarea to Jerusalem.

name: Morianton
bio: A Jaredite king, descendant of Riplakish, who gathered an army of outcasts and overthrew the ruling king. He established himself as king and dealt justly with the people, though he was personally immoral.

name: Morianton
bio: A Nephite leader who tried to lead his people to the land northward after a dispute. Captain Moroni sent Teancum to intercept him, and he was killed. He had been a violent man who beat one of his female servants.

name: Moron
bio: Jaredite king, son of Ethem. He was overthrown and dwelt in captivity all the remainder of his days. He was the father of Coriantor and grandfather of Ether the prophet.

name: Mulek
bio: A son of King Zedekiah of Judah who escaped the Babylonian destruction of Jerusalem and was led to the Americas. His descendants, the Mulekites (people of Zarahemla), were discovered by Mosiah I. Though Mulek himself is not mentioned as a speaking character, his lineage is significant in Book of Mormon geography and history.

name: Muloki
bio: One of the missionary companions of Aaron son of Mosiah who preached to the Lamanites. He was imprisoned in the land of Middoni along with Ammah.

name: Naamah
bio: Daughter of Lamech (descendant of Cain) and Zillah, and sister of Tubal-cain. One of the few antediluvian women named in Genesis.

name: Naamah
bio: Daughter of Zillah and Lamech, and sister of Tubal Cain (Moses 5:46).

name: Naaman
bio: A commander of the Syrian army who had leprosy. The Israelite prophet Elisha told him to wash seven times in the Jordan River to be healed. After initial reluctance (he expected something more dramatic), he obeyed and was healed. Jesus referenced his healing to illustrate that prophets are not always honored among their own people.

name: Nabal
bio: A wealthy but foolish man of Carmel married to Abigail. He refused to give provisions to David's men despite their protection of his flocks. He died of apparent stroke ten days after learning of Abigail's intervention.

name: Nadab (king of Israel)
bio: Son of Jeroboam I who reigned over Israel two years. He continued his father's sins and was assassinated by Baasha during a siege of Gibbethon.

name: Nahor
bio: Brother of Abraham and son of Terah. His wife Milcah bore him eight sons. He is the grandfather of Rebekah through his son Bethuel.

name: Nahor
bio: Brother of Abraham who married Milcah, the daughter of Haran. Named in Abraham 2:2.

name: Nahum
bio: A prophet from Elkosh who prophesied the destruction of Nineveh, the capital of Assyria. His prophecy, delivered about a century after Jonah's mission to Nineveh, declared that God's patience with the cruel Assyrian empire had ended.

name: Naphtali
bio: Sixth son of Jacob, born to Bilhah (Rachel's handmaid). His tribe settled in the region of Galilee, where much of Jesus' ministry would later occur.

name: Narcissus
bio: A man in Rome whose household members 'which are in the Lord' Paul greets in Romans 16:11.

name: Nathanael
bio: Referred to in D&C 41:11 in a metaphorical comparison, where the Lord says of Edward Partridge that he is like Nathanael of old, in whom there is no guile.

name: Nebuzaradan
bio: Captain of Nebuchadnezzar's guard who oversaw the destruction of Jerusalem and the temple. He deported the people to Babylon but released Jeremiah, allowing him to stay in the land.

name: Nephihah
bio: The second chief judge of the Nephites, appointed by Alma the Younger when he stepped down to preach. Nephihah was a just man who served faithfully until his death. He was succeeded by his son Pahoran.

name: Nereus
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:15 along with his unnamed sister.

name: Neum
bio: An Israelite prophet mentioned in 1 Nephi 19:10 whose writings were on the brass plates. He testified that the God of Israel would be crucified. His writings are not found in the current Bible.

name: Newel K. Whitney
bio: Second Presiding Bishop of the Church. He owned a store in Kirtland, Ohio, where Joseph Smith lived and received many revelations. He is mentioned in several D&C sections and served faithfully as bishop during challenging periods of Church history.

name: Newel Knight
bio: Early Latter-day Saint who experienced one of the first miracles of the Restoration when Joseph Smith cast out an evil spirit from him. Called in D&C 52 and 54, and named to the Nauvoo high council in D&C 124.

name: Nimrah
bio: Son of Akish who was angry because his father had killed his brother. He gathered a small number of men and fled to dwell with Omer.

name: Noah (Jaredite)
bio: Jaredite rebel, son of Corihor, who rebelled against King Shule. He drew away Cohor and gave battle to Shule, capturing him briefly before Shule's sons rescued him.

name: Noah Packard
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:136 as a counselor to preside over the quorum of high priests alongside Amasa Lyman.

name: Northrop Sweet
bio: Early convert addressed in D&C 33 alongside Ezra Thayre, called to open his mouth and declare the gospel like the sound of a trump.

name: Nymphas
bio: A believer in Laodicea (or nearby) in whose house a church met. Paul sends greetings in Colossians 4:15.

name: Obadiah
bio: Author of the shortest book in the Old Testament. He prophesied judgment against Edom (descendants of Esau) for their pride and for their hostility toward Judah when Jerusalem was attacked.

name: Obed
bio: Son of Boaz and Ruth. The women of Bethlehem praised God for his birth, and Naomi became his nurse. He was the father of Jesse and grandfather of David, placing him in the line of Christ.

name: Oholiab
bio: Son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan. He was appointed alongside Bezalel to work on the construction of the tabernacle, skilled in engraving, designing, and embroidering.

name: Olympas
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:15.

name: Omner
bio: Son of King Mosiah and one of the four missionary sons who served among the Lamanites. He was initially a rebel with Alma the Younger before their collective conversion by an angel. He spent fourteen years as a missionary among the Lamanites.

name: Omni
bio: Son of Jarom and author of the first portion of the Book of Omni. He described himself as a wicked man who had not kept the commandments but fulfilled his duty to keep the record. He passed the plates to his son Amaron.

name: Onan
bio: Second son of Judah. After his brother Er's death, he was told to perform the duty of a brother-in-law to Tamar but refused, and God put him to death.

name: Onesiphorus
bio: A believer praised by Paul in 2 Timothy 1:16-18 for often refreshing him and not being ashamed of his chains. He sought Paul out diligently when in Rome and found him.

name: Orihah
bio: Son of Jared and the first king of the Jaredites. He was anointed king despite his father and the brother of Jared having refused the monarchy. He reigned righteously and lived to an old age.

name: Orpah
bio: Moabite daughter-in-law of Naomi, wife of Chilion. When Naomi urged her daughters-in-law to return to Moab, Orpah kissed her goodbye and went back, while Ruth chose to stay.

name: Orson Spencer
bio: An educated convert who gave up a career as a Baptist minister to join the Church. He became chancellor of the University of Deseret (now University of Utah) and served a mission to England.

name: Paanchi
bio: Son of Pahoran who contended for the judgment-seat against his brothers Pahoran and Pacumeni. When he lost, he sought to incite rebellion and was condemned to death.

name: Pachus
bio: Leader of the king-men who drove the freemen and Pahoran out of Zarahemla. He was killed in battle when Captain Moroni and Pahoran retook the city.

name: Pacumeni
bio: Son of Pahoran who contended for the judgment-seat. After losing to his brother Pahoran, he united with the people's voice. He was later appointed chief judge after his brother's assassination and was killed when Coriantumr invaded Zarahemla.

name: Pagag
bio: Firstborn son of the brother of Jared. He was chosen by the people to be king but refused the office.

name: Paltiel
bio: Son of Laish to whom Saul gave Michal, David's wife, after David fled. When David demanded Michal back, Abner took her from Paltiel, who followed weeping until sent home.

name: Pashhur
bio: Chief officer of the temple who had Jeremiah beaten and put in stocks for prophesying Jerusalem's destruction. Jeremiah renamed him Magor-missabib ('Terror on Every Side') and prophesied his captivity.

name: Patrobas
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:14.

name: Pekah
bio: King of Israel who seized the throne by assassinating Pekahiah. He allied with Rezin of Syria against Judah. Tiglath-pileser conquered much of his territory. He was assassinated by Hoshea.

name: Pekahiah
bio: King of Israel who reigned two years and continued in the sins of Jeroboam. His officer Pekah conspired against him and assassinated him in the citadel of Samaria.

name: Peninnah
bio: Second wife of Elkanah who had children. She provoked and taunted Hannah for her barrenness, driving Hannah to tearful prayer that led to Samuel's birth.

name: Persis
bio: A beloved woman in the Roman church whom Paul greets in Romans 16:12, noting she 'laboured much in the Lord.'

name: Peter Haws
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to organize with George Miller, Lyman Wight, and John Snider to build the Nauvoo House.

name: Peter Whitmer Jr.
bio: One of the Eight Witnesses of the Book of Mormon and brother of David and John Whitmer. The Church was organized at his parents' home in Fayette, New York on April 6, 1830. He served a mission to the Lamanites (Native Americans) and died in 1836.

name: Philemon
bio: A wealthy Christian in Colossae to whom Paul wrote a personal letter asking him to receive back his runaway slave Onesimus — not as a slave but as a brother in Christ. Paul's epistle to Philemon is the shortest of his letters.

name: Philetus
bio: A false teacher associated with Hymenaeus who taught that the resurrection had already occurred, overthrowing the faith of some believers.

name: Philip Burroughs
bio: An early church member mentioned in D&C 30 as the brother at whose home John Whitmer was to labor and preach in the surrounding region.

name: Philologus
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:15 alongside Julia.

name: Phinehas (son of Eli)
bio: Son of Eli the priest. He and his brother Hophni were corrupt priests who abused their office. Both were killed when the Philistines captured the ark, and his wife died in childbirth upon hearing the news.

name: Phlegon
bio: A believer in Rome greeted by Paul in Romans 16:14.

name: Phygellus
bio: Named in 2 Timothy 1:15 alongside Hermogenes as one of those in Asia who turned away from Paul.

name: Potiphera
bio: Priest of On (Heliopolis) in Egypt. His daughter Asenath was given to Joseph as a wife by Pharaoh.

name: Priscilla
bio: Wife of Aquila and an early Christian leader who, with her husband, was a tentmaker and coworker of Paul. They helped instruct Apollos more accurately in the faith. Paul praised them as those who 'laid down their own necks' for his life.

name: Publius
bio: The chief man of the island of Malta who received Paul and his companions hospitably for three days after their shipwreck. Paul healed his father of a fever and dysentery.

name: Pudens
bio: A believer who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul in 2 Timothy 4:21.

name: Quartus
bio: A Christian brother who sends greetings to the Roman church in Romans 16:23.

name: Rabshakeh
bio: A high Assyrian official sent by Sennacherib to demand Jerusalem's surrender. He spoke in Hebrew to demoralize the people, mocking their trust in God. An angel then destroyed 185,000 Assyrian soldiers.

name: Raphael
bio: An angel mentioned in D&C 128:21 as one of the heavenly messengers who appeared in the current dispensation, along with Michael, Gabriel, and others.

name: Reuben
bio: Firstborn son of Jacob and Leah. He lost his birthright for an offense against his father. He tried to save Joseph from being sold by suggesting they put him in a pit rather than kill him. His tribe settled east of the Jordan River.

name: Reynolds Cahoon
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey to Missouri with Samuel H. Smith. He served faithfully in various church callings.

name: Rezin
bio: King of Aram-Damascus who allied with Pekah of Israel against Judah during the Syro-Ephraimite crisis. Isaiah prophesied his defeat, and Tiglath-pileser of Assyria killed him and conquered Damascus.

name: Riplakish
bio: A wicked Jaredite king who did not do that which was right. He taxed the people heavily, built many prisons, and had many wives and concubines. He was eventually overthrown and killed by his own people.

name: Rizpah
bio: Concubine of King Saul. After David gave her two sons to the Gibeonites to be hanged, she guarded their exposed bodies for months, keeping birds and beasts away. Her devotion moved David to properly bury Saul's descendants.

name: Robert B. Thompson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to help Joseph Smith write a proclamation. The Lord said he was well pleased with him.

name: Robert D. Foster
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to build a house for Joseph Smith. He was told to obey the Lord's voice and hearken to counsel. He later apostatized.

name: Rufus
bio: Greeted by Paul in Romans 16:13 as 'chosen in the Lord,' with his mother also greeted warmly. Possibly the son of Simon of Cyrene mentioned in Mark 15:21.

name: Ruggles Eames
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Stephen Burnett.

name: Salome (daughter of Herodias)
bio: One of the women present at Jesus' crucifixion and who came to anoint his body on Easter morning. Named alongside Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James in Mark 15:40 and 16:1.

name: Samuel Bent
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: Samuel Rolfe
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:142 with his counselors for the priests quorum.

name: Samuel Smith
bio: Younger brother of Joseph Smith and one of the Eight Witnesses of the Book of Mormon. He served one of the first missions for the Church, distributing copies of the Book of Mormon. He died shortly after the martyrdom of Joseph and Hyrum.

name: Samuel Williams
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:137 to preside over the quorum of elders alongside John A. Hicks and Jesse Baker.

name: Sceva
bio: A Jewish chief priest in Ephesus whose seven sons attempted to cast out evil spirits by invoking the name of Jesus. The evil spirit overpowered them, saying 'Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are ye?'

name: Seantum
bio: Brother of the chief judge Seezoram whom he murdered. Nephi son of Helaman prophetically revealed his guilt, and Seantum confessed when confronted.

name: Secundus
bio: A Thessalonian believer who accompanied Paul on his journey from Greece to Jerusalem, named alongside Aristarchus in Acts 20:4.

name: Seezoram
bio: Chief judge of the Nephites who was murdered by his brother Seantum. His assassination was revealed through the prophetic words of Nephi son of Helaman.

name: Selah J. Griffin
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to be ordained and take his journey to Missouri alongside Newel Knight.

name: Sergius Paulus
bio: The Roman proconsul of Cyprus described as a prudent man. He sent for Barnabas and Saul to hear the word of God and believed after witnessing the blinding of Elymas the sorcerer.

name: Servant Girl of Naaman's Wife
bio: A young Israelite girl captured from Israel who served Naaman's wife. She told her mistress about the prophet Elisha in Samaria, initiating Naaman's journey to be healed of leprosy.

name: Seth
bio: Third named son of Adam and Eve, born after Abel's death. He was in the likeness of Adam and became the progenitor of the line leading to Noah. The Pearl of Great Price describes him as a righteous man who was ordained by Adam.

name: Seth (Jaredite)
bio: Jaredite who was brought into captivity all his days. Father of Ahah. Not to be confused with the biblical Seth.

name: Seymour Brunson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Daniel Stanton. Named to the Nauvoo high council in D&C 124 but noted as having been taken by the Lord.

name: Shadrach Roundy
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:141 to preside over the bishopric alongside Vinson Knight and Samuel H. Smith.

name: Shallum (king of Israel)
bio: Assassinated King Zechariah and took the throne but reigned only one month before Menahem attacked Samaria and killed him.

name: Shalmaneser V
bio: King of Assyria who besieged Samaria for three years after King Hoshea stopped paying tribute. The northern kingdom of Israel fell during his reign.

name: Shaphan
bio: Secretary to King Josiah who received the Book of the Law when Hilkiah found it during temple repairs. He read it to Josiah, prompting the king's great reforms.

name: Shared
bio: Jaredite warrior who fought against Coriantumr during the final Jaredite wars. He gave battle to Coriantumr and defeated him, driving him to the valley of Gilgal before being overthrown.

name: Shear-jashub
bio: Son of Isaiah whose prophetic name means 'A remnant shall return.' Isaiah brought him to meet King Ahaz during the Syro-Ephraimite crisis as a living sign of God's promise.

name: Sheba
bio: A Benjaminite who led a rebellion against David after Absalom's defeat, declaring 'We have no portion in David.' Joab pursued him to Abel Beth-maacah, where a wise woman persuaded the city to throw Sheba's head over the wall.

name: Shechem
bio: Son of Hamor the Hivite. He violated Dinah, daughter of Jacob, then sought to marry her. Simeon and Levi deceived his city into being circumcised, then killed all the men including Shechem in revenge.

name: Shelah
bio: Third son of Judah and his Canaanite wife. Judah withheld him from marrying Tamar after the deaths of his brothers Er and Onan, fearing Shelah too would die.

name: Shem
bio: Son of Noah who survived the flood and became the ancestor of the Semitic peoples. He was blessed by Noah and his tent was a place of righteousness. In LDS tradition, some identify him with Melchizedek, though this is not canonized doctrine.

name: Shemnon
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his appearance to the Nephites after his resurrection.

name: Shez
bio: Jaredite descendant who survived the destruction caused by war and began to rebuild the people. He remembered the destruction and established a righteous kingdom.

name: Shiblom
bio: Nephite military commander at the final battle of Cumorah who led ten thousand men and fell in battle. Not to be confused with the Jaredite king Shiblom.

name: Shiblom (Jaredite)
bio: Jaredite king whose brother rebelled against him. During his reign many prophets testified and were rejected, and a great curse came upon the land.

name: Shiblon
bio: Second son of Alma the Younger, praised by his father for his faithfulness and steadiness. He served as a missionary and briefly held the sacred records between Helaman's death and passing them to Helaman's son. Alma compared him favorably to his troubled brother Corianton.

name: Shishak
bio: Pharaoh of Egypt who invaded Judah during Rehoboam's reign and carried off the treasures of the temple and palace, including the golden shields Solomon had made.

name: Shiz
bio: A Jaredite military leader who fought against Coriantumr in the final Jaredite wars. He pursued Coriantumr relentlessly, and the two sides destroyed virtually the entire Jaredite civilization in their conflict. He was the last opponent Coriantumr faced and was killed by him.

name: Sidney Gilbert
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 53 to forsake the world and establish a store in Missouri. He served as a purchasing agent for the church in Zion.

name: Silas
bio: A leader in the Jerusalem church who accompanied Paul on his second missionary journey. He and Paul were imprisoned in Philippi, where an earthquake freed them. He is also associated with Peter and may have assisted in writing 1 Peter.

name: Simeon
bio: Second son of Jacob and Leah. He and Levi massacred the men of Shechem to avenge their sister Dinah. Jacob prophesied that his tribe would be scattered in Israel. His tribe was eventually absorbed into the territory of Judah.

name: Simeon
bio: A devout man in Jerusalem who was promised by the Holy Ghost that he would not die before seeing the Messiah. When Mary and Joseph brought the infant Jesus to the temple, Simeon took the child in his arms and prophesied, including that a sword would pierce Mary's soul.

name: Simeon (called Niger)
bio: One of the prophets and teachers in the church at Antioch, listed alongside Barnabas, Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen in Acts 13:1.

name: Simeon Carter
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey to Missouri with Solomon Hancock. Also paired with Emer Harris in D&C 75.

name: Simon (brother of Jesus)
bio: Named as one of Jesus' brothers in Matthew 13:55 and Mark 6:3, alongside James, Joses, and Judas.

name: Simon the Leper
bio: A man in Bethany at whose house a woman anointed Jesus with expensive ointment shortly before his crucifixion.

name: Simon the Zealot
bio: One of the original twelve apostles. Called 'the Zealot' (or 'Canaanite,' an Aramaic equivalent), suggesting he may have been associated with the Zealot movement before following Jesus. Little is recorded about his specific ministry.

name: Simonds Ryder
bio: Early Latter-day Saint who received the calling that was taken from Heman Basset as mentioned in D&C 52. He later apostatized.

name: Solomon Hancock
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey to Missouri with Simeon Carter, preaching along the way.

name: Solomon Humphrey
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey to the eastern lands with Joseph Wakefield.

name: Sopater
bio: A man from Berea who accompanied Paul from Greece on his journey to Jerusalem. Possibly the same as Sosipater whom Paul calls a kinsman in Romans 16:21.

name: Sophronia Smith
bio: Older sister of Joseph Smith, named in Joseph Smith--History 1:4 as one of the eleven children of Joseph Smith Sr. and Lucy Mack Smith.

name: Sosthenes
bio: Chief ruler of the Corinthian synagogue who was beaten before Gallio's judgment seat. Likely the same Sosthenes Paul later calls 'our brother' in the greeting of 1 Corinthians.

name: Stachys
bio: A believer in Rome whom Paul greets as 'my beloved' in Romans 16:9.

name: Stephanas
bio: A Corinthian believer whose household was the firstfruits of Achaia and who devoted themselves to the ministry. Paul personally baptized his household and was glad when Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus came to him.

name: Stephen Burnett
bio: An early Church member called on a mission in D&C 80. He later left the Church during the Kirtland apostasy of 1838.

name: Susanna
bio: One of the women who ministered to Jesus and the twelve apostles from their own substance, named alongside Joanna and Mary Magdalene in Luke 8:3.

name: Sylvester Smith
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 75 to be united in the ministry with Gideon Carter. He participated in Zion's Camp.

name: Syntyche
bio: A woman in the Philippian church whom Paul beseeches to be of the same mind as Euodia, noting that both had labored with him in the gospel.

name: Tamar
bio: Daughter-in-law of Judah who disguised herself as a prostitute to secure offspring through Judah after he failed to provide his third son as her husband. She bore twins Perez and Zerah. She appears in the genealogy of Jesus Christ in Matthew 1.

name: Tattenai
bio: Persian governor of the province Beyond the River who questioned the Jews' authority to rebuild the temple. He reported to Darius, who found Cyrus's decree and ordered Tattenai to support the construction.

name: Teomner
bio: Nephite military officer who served under Helaman during the war chapters. He and Gid executed a decoy strategy to retake the city of Manti from the Lamanites.

name: Terah
bio: Father of Abraham, Nahor, and Haran. He departed Ur of the Chaldeans with Abraham and Lot, intending to go to Canaan, but settled in Haran where he died at age 205.

name: Tertius
bio: Paul's amanuensis who physically wrote the Epistle to the Romans. He adds his own greeting in Romans 16:22: 'I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.'

name: Tertullus
bio: A Roman orator hired by the high priest Ananias and the Jewish elders to prosecute Paul before Governor Felix in Caesarea.

name: Theudas
bio: A rebel leader mentioned by Gamaliel in Acts 5:36 who boasted of being somebody and drew about 400 followers before being killed, after which his movement scattered.

name: Thomas B. Marsh
bio: First president of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles. He received a personal revelation (D&C 31) and was told to 'be patient in afflictions.' He later apostatized over a dispute but returned to the Church in 1857 and bore testimony of its truthfulness.

name: Thomas Grover
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: Tiberius Caesar
bio: The Roman emperor during whose fifteenth year of reign John the Baptist began preaching and Jesus began his ministry.

name: Tiglath-pileser III
bio: Powerful king of Assyria who conquered much of Syria and Israel. King Ahaz of Judah appealed to him for help against Syria and Israel, paying heavy tribute from the temple treasury.

name: Timothy
bio: Brother of Nephi (3 Nephi) and one of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus during his visit to the Americas. He was raised from the dead by his brother Nephi after being killed.

name: Titius Justus
