bio: A God-fearing man in Corinth whose house was next to the synagogue. Paul used his home as a base for teaching after leaving the synagogue.

name: Titus
bio: A Greek convert who was a trusted companion of Paul. Paul sent him to Corinth to help resolve problems and later left him in Crete to organize churches. Paul's epistle to Titus provides instructions on church leadership.

name: Titus Billings
bio: Early Latter-day Saint mentioned in D&C 63:39, commanded regarding the disposition of his property in Zion.

name: Trophimus
bio: An Ephesian Christian who accompanied Paul to Jerusalem, where his presence sparked a riot when Jews assumed Paul had brought him into the temple. Paul later left him sick at Miletus.

name: Tryphena
bio: A woman in the Roman church greeted by Paul in Romans 16:12 alongside Tryphosa as one who labors in the Lord.

name: Tryphosa
bio: A woman in the Roman church greeted by Paul in Romans 16:12 alongside Tryphena as one who labors in the Lord.

name: Tubal-cain
bio: Son of Lamech (descendant of Cain) and Zillah. He was a forger of all instruments of bronze and iron, the first metalworker mentioned in scripture.

name: Tubaloth
bio: King of the Lamanites who was the son of Ammoron. He appointed Coriantumr to lead an army against the Nephites in an invasion of Zarahemla.

name: Tychicus
bio: A trusted companion of Paul from Asia who accompanied him on his journey to Jerusalem. Paul calls him 'a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord' and sent him to Ephesus and Colossae as a letter-carrier and encourager.

name: Tyrannus
bio: Owner of a lecture hall in Ephesus where Paul taught daily for two years after leaving the synagogue.

name: Urbanus
bio: A fellow worker in Christ greeted by Paul in Romans 16:9.

name: Vienna Jaques
bio: Early Latter-day Saint woman addressed in D&C 90:28-31, commanded to receive money to bear her expenses and go to the land of Zion. She donated much of her savings to the church.

name: Vinson Knight
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124 to put stock into the Nauvoo House and also called to preside over the bishopric along with Samuel H. Smith and Shadrach Roundy.

name: W. W. Phelps
bio: An early convert who served as printer, editor, and hymn writer for the Church. He apostatized and testified against Joseph Smith in Missouri but later repented and was forgiven. Joseph Smith's famous letter to him, 'Come on, dear brother, since the war is past,' is preserved. He wrote the hymn 'The Spirit of God.'

name: Warren A. Cowdery
bio: Older brother of Oliver Cowdery, called in D&C 106 to be a presiding high priest over the church in Freedom, New York. The Lord said there was joy in heaven when he bowed to the Lord's scepter.

name: Wheeler Baldwin
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey with William Carter.

name: Willard Richards
bio: An apostle and Church historian who was present at Carthage Jail when Joseph and Hyrum Smith were killed. He was the only one of the four men present who was not wounded, fulfilling Joseph Smith's earlier prophecy. He later served as second counselor to Brigham Young.

name: William Carter
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey with Wheeler Baldwin.

name: William Huntington
bio: Early Latter-day Saint named in D&C 124:132 as a member of the Nauvoo high council.

name: William Marks
bio: Early Latter-day Saint stake president in Nauvoo. Called in D&C 117 to preside and in D&C 124 to pay stock into the Nauvoo House and to ordain Isaac Galland.

name: William McLellin
bio: One of the original members of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles. He received several revelations through Joseph Smith (D&C 66, 68). He later apostatized and was excommunicated. He attempted to test Joseph's prophetic gift by secretly preparing questions, which Joseph answered by revelation.

name: Zebedee
bio: Father of the apostles James and John. He was a fisherman on the Sea of Galilee and was in the boat mending nets when Jesus called his sons to follow him.

name: Zebedee Coltrin
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 52 to take his journey with Levi W. Hancock. He served in the first quorum of seventy.

name: Zebulun
bio: Tenth son of Jacob and Leah. Jacob prophesied that Zebulun would dwell at the haven of the sea and be a haven for ships.

name: Zechariah (king of Israel)
bio: Son of Jeroboam II and last king of the dynasty of Jehu. He reigned only six months before Shallum assassinated him publicly, fulfilling God's promise that Jehu's dynasty would last four generations.

name: Zedekiah
bio: One of the twelve Nephite disciples chosen by Jesus Christ during his visit to the Americas. He shares the name with the last king of Judah but is a distinct individual.

name: Zelophehad
bio: A man of the tribe of Manasseh who died in the wilderness with no sons. His five daughters petitioned Moses for an inheritance, establishing the legal precedent that daughters could inherit when there were no sons.

name: Zemnarihah
bio: Gadianton robber leader who besieged the Nephites after Giddianhi was killed. When his siege failed because the Nephites had gathered provisions, he attempted to retreat but was captured and hanged.

name: Zenas
bio: A lawyer whom Paul asks Titus to help on his journey, along with Apollos, so that nothing is wanting for them.

name: Zenephi
bio: A Lamanite leader mentioned in Mormon's epistle to his son Moroni. Mormon reported that Zenephi's army had taken many Nephite women and children prisoner.

name: Zenock
bio: An Israelite prophet quoted in the Book of Mormon whose writings were on the brass plates. He prophesied of Christ's coming and was slain for his testimony. He is mentioned alongside Zenos and Neum as brass plate prophets not found in the current Bible.

name: Zephaniah
bio: A prophet during the reign of King Josiah who warned of the coming 'day of the Lord' — a time of worldwide judgment. He prophesied against Judah's idolatry and the surrounding nations but concluded with a message of hope for a restored remnant.

name: Zera Pulsipher
bio: Early Latter-day Saint called in D&C 124:138 to preside over the quorum of seventies. He was one of the first seven presidents of the Seventy.

name: Zeram
bio: One of four Nephite spies sent by Alma to watch the camp of the Amlicites. He and his companions reported back on the combined Amlicite-Lamanite army.

name: Zeresh
bio: Wife of Haman who advised him to build a 75-foot gallows for Mordecai. After Haman was forced to honor Mordecai, she warned that if Mordecai was Jewish, Haman would surely fall.

name: Zeruiah
bio: David's sister and mother of the three warriors Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. She is consistently identified as the mother of these men, suggesting her prominence in the family.

name: Ziba
bio: Servant of the house of Saul whom David appointed to manage Mephibosheth's estate. During Absalom's rebellion, Ziba brought provisions to David and falsely claimed Mephibosheth had stayed behind hoping for the throne.

name: Ziba Peterson
bio: Early Latter-day Saint missionary called in D&C 32 to accompany Oliver Cowdery, Parley P. Pratt, and Peter Whitmer Jr. on the mission to the Lamanites.

name: Zillah
bio: One of the two wives of Lamech (descendant of Cain). She bore Tubal Cain, an instructor of artificers in brass and iron, and a daughter named Naamah (Moses 5:44-46).

name: Zimri
bio: Chariot commander who assassinated King Elah and reigned only seven days. When the army proclaimed Omri king and besieged Tirzah, Zimri burned the palace down on himself.

name: Amoz
bio: The father of the prophet Isaiah.

name: Remaliah
bio: The father of Pekah, king of Israel, who conspired with Syria against Judah during the Syro-Ephraimite War.

name: Jeberechiah
bio: The father of Zechariah, one of the faithful witnesses called by Isaiah to verify his prophecy regarding Maher-shalal-hash-baz.

name: Tabeal
bio: An individual whose unnamed son was proposed as a puppet king of Judah by the Syrian-Israelite coalition during the Syro-Ephraimite War.

name: Jotham (king of Judah)
bio: King of Judah, son of Uzziah. His son Ahaz succeeded him during the Syro-Ephraimite crisis.

name: Aaron (Lamanite king)
bio: The king of the Lamanites who led an army of forty and four thousand against Mormon and was defeated.

name: Priest of Elkenah
bio: The idolatrous priest in Chaldea who attempted to sacrifice Abraham on the altar before being struck down by Jehovah.

name: Sisinnes
bio: Governor of Syria and Phenice who questioned the Jews about rebuilding the temple.

name: Sathrabuzanes
bio: A companion of Sisinnes who opposed the building of the temple.

name: Belemus
bio: An official who wrote to Artaxerxes to stop the Jews from building.

name: Rehum
bio: The chancellor who wrote against the Jews to Artaxerxes.

name: Artaxerxes
bio: King of Persia who authorized Ezra to lead a group of Jews back to Jerusalem and granted him authority to enforce the Law of Moses.

name: Zerubbabel
bio: A descendant of King David who led the first group of Jewish exiles back to Jerusalem and oversaw the rebuilding of the temple.

name: Darius
bio: King of Persia who confirmed Cyrus's decree and authorized the completion of the second temple in Jerusalem.

name: Antiochus Epiphanes
bio: Seleucid king who persecuted the Jews and desecrated the temple.

name: Mattathias
bio: Priest who started the Maccabean revolt against the Seleucid Empire.

name: Judas Maccabeus
bio: Son of Mattathias and primary leader of the revolt.

name: Jonathan
bio: Son of Mattathias who succeeded Judas as leader and high priest.

name: Simon
bio: Son of Mattathias who secured Judean independence.

name: Alexander Balas
bio: Claimant to the Seleucid throne who befriended Jonathan.

name: Demetrius
bio: Seleucid king who struggled against Judas and Jonathan.

name: Trypho
bio: General who treacherously captured Jonathan and usurped the throne.

name: Nicanor
bio: Seleucid general defeated by Judas Maccabeus.

name: Alcimus
bio: Hellenizing high priest who opposed the Maccabees.

name: Uriel
bio: The archangel sent to answer Ezra's questions and deliver divine revelations throughout the visions of 2 Esdras.

name: Heliodorus
bio: The treasurer of Seleucus IV who was prevented by a vision from robbing the temple.

name: Onias
bio: The pious High Priest who was eventually murdered through the intrigue of Menelaus.

name: Eleazar
bio: An elderly scribe who chose martyrdom over violating the law of God.

name: Lysias
bio: A Seleucid general and regent who fought against and later made peace with the Jews.

name: Mother of the Seven Brothers
bio: An unnamed woman who encouraged her seven sons to accept martyrdom for their faith.

name: Razis
bio: An elder of Jerusalem who died by suicide to avoid falling into the hands of the enemy.

name: Aman
bio: The king's chief minister (Haman) who plotted to destroy the Jewish people.

name: Judith
bio: A pious widow of Bethulia who delivered Israel by beheading Holofernes.

name: Holofernes
bio: The chief captain of Nebuchadnezzar's army who besieged Bethulia.

name: Achior
bio: Captain of the Ammonites who warned Holofernes and later joined the house of Israel.

name: Ozias
bio: The governor of Bethulia during the Assyrian siege.

name: Arphaxad
bio: A king of the Medes defeated by Nebuchadnezzar.

name: Bagoas
bio: The eunuch and servant of Holofernes.

name: Jesus son of Sirach
bio: The author and narrator of the book of Sirach, a Jewish scribe who collected wisdom in Jerusalem.

name: Simon the High Priest
bio: Likely Simon II (the Just), described as a great high priest who fortified the temple and the city and whose appearance in temple ritual is praised extensively.

name: Susanna
bio: The protagonist; daughter of Chelcias and wife of Joacim, known for her beauty, righteousness, and refusal to sin against God even under threat of death.

name: Joacim
bio: A wealthy and honorable Jew living in Babylon, the husband of Susanna.

name: Chelcias
bio: The father of Susanna, noted for being a righteous man who taught his daughter according to the Law of Moses.

name: Tobit
bio: A righteous man of the tribe of Naphtali, living in captivity in Nineveh, known for his almsgiving and burial of the dead.

name: Anna
bio: The wife of Tobit and mother of Tobias.

name: Tobias
bio: The son of Tobit and Anna, who undergoes a journey to Media guided by the angel Raphael.

name: Sarah
bio: The daughter of Raguel in Media, afflicted by a demon but later healed and married to Tobias.

name: Raguel
bio: A kinsman of Tobit living in Ecbatana, the father of Sarah.

name: Asmodeus
bio: An evil spirit or demon who killed Sarah's seven husbands and was later banished by Tobias.

name: Angel Raphael
bio: One of the seven holy angels who presents the prayers of the saints; he disguised himself as Azarias to guide Tobias.

name: Edna
bio: The wife of Raguel and mother of Sarah.

name: Alexander
bio: Mentioned alongside Hymenaeus for making shipwreck of his faith and being delivered unto Satan.
